_comment: Bonsai ascension-mark bake manifest. Sixteen unique heraldic ornaments per the §A2 + §B3 + bottom-row decision. Mark N unlocks at hero level ceil(100 * N / 16). The runtime compositor renders 16 fixed slots along the bottom inside the frame; lit slots draw the baked PNG tinted to the hero's tier accent, unlit slots draw a faint cairn-stone outline placeholder. See docs/design/HERO_PORTRAITS.md.
preamble: Single small heraldic ornament — one isolated symbolic mark centered on the canvas, no scene, no figure, no character.

style.mark: Style: a single bold heraldic ornament rendered as solid antique gold (#C9A961) on a pure solid white (#FFFFFF) background. ABSOLUTELY no figure, no character, no person, no face, no text, no letters, no numbers — only the symbolic ornament. Medieval heraldic engraving style. Bold, simple silhouette readable at small size (no fine engraving detail). Single-weight strokes or solid filled shapes, no shading, no gradient. Symmetrical about the vertical axis.

tail: Composition: perfectly centered, square 1:1, the ornament fills the central 70% of the frame on a clean uniform solid white background. Ornament only — no text, no letters, no numbers, no clutter.

marks.subject: A single short horizontal heraldic notch or tally mark, austere and simple.
A single small heraldic laurel sprig with three pairs of leaves, a humble victory token.
Two short heraldic swords crossed in a saltire, simple and martial.
A single small heraldic interlaced knot, a Celtic-style endless braid.
A heraldic cross pattée — a Greek cross with arms broadening outward at the tips.
A heraldic fleur-de-lis, the classic stylized three-petalled lily.
A heraldic six-pointed star, the Star of David's points without filled center, just outlined arms.
A small closed heraldic laurel wreath, a complete circular wreath of laurel leaves.
A heraldic sunburst — a small central disc with twelve alternating long-and-short rays radiating outward.
A heraldic patriarchal cross — a Latin cross with a second shorter horizontal arm above the main crossbar.
A heraldic Tudor rose — a stylized open rose with five large petals seen from the front.
A heraldic lion's head silhouette in profile facing left, with the lion's eye obscured by deep mane shadow so no facial features are visible — only the silhouette outline.
A heraldic crown — a five-point coronet with stylized jewel insets along the band, drawn in crimson and bright gold.
A heraldic winged sword — a vertical sword with feathered wings spread to either side of the hilt, drawn in crimson and bright gold.
A heraldic spread eagle facing forward, wings fully outstretched and tail fanned, with the eagle's head turned away so no facial features are visible, drawn in crimson and bright gold.
A grand heraldic eight-pointed compass star with a central jewel cabochon and elaborate filigree between the points, the most ornate of all heraldic ornaments, drawn in crimson and bright gold.
